edges.description: Pathogenic variants impair the encoded lysosomal membrane transporter.
Reduced sialin export traps free sialic acid in lysosomes.
Lysosomal sialic acid storage is associated with altered GSL profiles and lysosomal glycohydrolase activity.
Lysosomal storage and downstream neural-cell dysfunction contribute to central white matter disease.
Severe infantile lysosomal storage disease is linked to coarse facial features as part of the multisystem FSASD phenotype.
Severe infantile lysosomal storage disease is connected to hepatosplenomegaly as part of the multisystem storage phenotype.
Severe infantile lysosomal storage disease is connected to cardiomegaly as part of the multisystem FSASD phenotype.
Severe infantile SLC17A5 loss can present prenatally with hydrops fetalis.
Altered neural lipid metabolism plausibly contributes to white matter vulnerability.
Neural-cell metabolic dysfunction contributes to developmental delay and progressive neurologic impairment.
Thinning of the corpus callosum is a structural neuroimaging manifestation of the same central white matter disease process.
Cerebellar atrophy is a neuroimaging component of FSASD central white matter/CNS involvement.
White matter disease contributes to developmental delay.
Cerebellar and white matter involvement contribute to impaired coordination.
Central motor pathway involvement contributes to spasticity.
Cognitive impairment is a core manifestation of FSASD neurodevelopmental and neurodegenerative dysfunction.
Muscular hypotonia is a neurologic manifestation of FSASD neurodevelopmental involvement.
Nystagmus is connected as part of the Salla disease neurologic phenotype cluster.
Athetosis is connected as part of the Salla disease neurologic movement phenotype cluster.
Epileptic seizures are connected as a downstream manifestation of progressive neurologic disease.
Psychosis is modeled as a later neuropsychiatric manifestation in the Salla disease spectrum.
Sleep disturbance is modeled as a psychiatric/behavioral downstream manifestation in Salla disease.
Aggressive behavior or restlessness is modeled as a behavioral downstream manifestation in Salla disease.
Elevated free sialic acid reports the upstream lysosomal N-acetylneuraminic acid storage burden.
Elevated CSF NAAG supports SLC17A5-related sialin transport dysfunction in hypomyelinating disease.
Altered glycosphingolipid levels report secondary GSL metabolic dysregulation in FSASD.

nodes.description: Aggressive behavior or restlessness can occur as a behavioral manifestation in Salla disease.

evidence.reference_title: Psychiatric symptoms in Salla disease.
evidence.snippet: aggressive behaviour disorders or restlessness (6/24, 25%)

nodes.description: Cerebellar ataxia contributes to motor impairment in FSASD, especially Salla disease.

evidence.reference_title: Free sialic acid storage disorder: Progress and promise.
evidence.snippet: Clinical features of FSASD include coarse facial features, organomegaly, and progressive neurodegenerative symptoms with cognitive impairment, cerebellar ataxia and muscular hypotonia.

nodes.description: Athetotic movements are a characteristic movement phenotype in Salla disease.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Less severe FSASD (historically called Salla disease) is characterized by normal appearance and absence of neurologic findings at birth, followed by slowly progressive neurologic deterioration resulting in mild-to-moderate psychomotor delays, spasticity, athetosis, and epileptic seizures.

nodes.description: Investigational adenine base editing has corrected the common SLC17A5 c.115C>T variant and reduced free sialic acid in patient-derived fibroblasts and mouse embryonic fibroblasts, but it is not an approved therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Base editing corrects the common Salla disease SLC17A5 c.115C>T variant.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, ABE treatment of either homozygous or compound heterozygous SLC17A5 c.115C>T human dermal fibroblasts demonstrated significant FSA reduction, supporting amelioration of disease pathology.

evidence.reference_title: Base editing corrects the common Salla disease SLC17A5 c.115C>T variant.
evidence.snippet: Our study demonstrates the feasibility of base editing as a therapeutic approach for the FSASD variant SLC17A5 c.115C>T and highlights the usefulness of base editing in monogenic diseases where transmembrane protein function is impaired.

nodes.description: Cardiomegaly can occur in severe infantile FSASD.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Severe FSASD (historically referred to as infantile free sialic acid storage disease, or ISSD) is characterized by severe developmental delay, coarse facial features, hepatosplenomegaly, and cardiomegaly; death usually occurs in early childhood.

subtypes: Infantile Free Sialic Acid Storage Disease

nodes.description: Cerebellar atrophy contributes to ataxia and progressive neurologic impairment.

evidence.reference_title: Free sialic acid storage disorder: Progress and promise.
evidence.snippet: Central hypomyelination with cerebellar atrophy and thinning of the corpus callosum are also prominent disease features.

nodes.description: Coarse facial features are most prominent in severe infantile FSASD.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Severe FSASD (historically referred to as infantile free sialic acid storage disease, or ISSD) is characterized by severe developmental delay, coarse facial features, hepatosplenomegaly, and cardiomegaly; death usually occurs in early childhood.

subtypes: Infantile Free Sialic Acid Storage Disease

nodes.description: Thinning or hypoplasia of the corpus callosum is a recurrent neuroimaging feature.

evidence.reference_title: Free sialic acid storage disorder: Progress and promise.
evidence.snippet: Central hypomyelination with cerebellar atrophy and thinning of the corpus callosum are also prominent disease features.

evidence.reference_title: Elevated CSF N-acetylaspartylglutamate in patients with free sialic acid storage diseases.
evidence.snippet: We found a significant increase of free sialic acid in CSF or urine in 6 of 41 patients presenting with hypomyelination of unknown etiology.

evidence.reference_title: Investigating the Utility of Leukocyte Sialic Acid Measurements in Lysosomal Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with FSASD exhibited significantly higher levels of free Neu5Ac compared to their unaffected biological parents (36-fold), LSD-negative subjects (22-fold), and individuals with other LSDs (49-fold).

readouts.interpretation: Elevated free sialic acid reports the upstream lysosomal N-acetylneuraminic acid storage burden.

nodes.description: Carrier testing and prenatal or preimplantation testing are relevant once familial SLC17A5 variants are known.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Once the SLC17A5 pathogenic variants have been identified in an affected family member, molecular genetic carrier testing and prenatal/preimplantation genetic testing are possible.

nodes.description: Developmental delay is a core presentation across the FSASD spectrum and can be severe in infantile disease.

evidence.reference_title: A New Patient With Intermediate Severe Salla Disease With Hypomyelination: A Literature Review for Salla Disease.
evidence.snippet: Likely pathogenic variants in SLC17A5 results in allelic disorders of free sialic acid metabolism including (1) infantile free sialic acid storage disease with severe global developmental delay, coarse facial features, hepatosplenomegaly, and cardiomegaly; (2) intermediate severe Salla disease with moderate to severe global developmental delay, hypotonia, and hypomyelination with or without coarse facial features, and (3) Salla disease with normal appearance, mild cognitive dysfunction, and spasticity.

evidence.reference_title: Longitudinal Characterization of the Clinical Course of Intermediate-Severe Salla Disease.
evidence.snippet: Disease onset occurred within the first six months of life in both patients. Early childhood development was delayed

nodes.description: Human CSF/plasma and patient-derived iPSC neural-cell studies show secondary glycosphingolipid dysregulation, especially in mature astrocytes, together with altered lysosomal glycohydrolase activity.

evidence.reference_title: Changes in glycosphingolipid levels in plasma and cerebrospinal fluid of individuals with Lysosomal Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: In CSF, total GSLs, GM1a, GM3, GD3, GD1a, and GD1b were significantly elevated compared to comparison samples.

evidence.reference_title: Lysosomal free sialic acid storage disorder iPSC-derived neural cells display altered glycosphingolipid metabolism.
evidence.snippet: We observed significant alterations in the abundance of specific GSL species, predominantly in mature astrocytes, with fewer changes in other cell types.

experimental_models.description: Patient-derived neural cells capture altered GSL abundance, especially in mature astrocytes.
experimental_models.name: Patient-derived iPSC neural cell models

evidence.reference_title: Changes in glycosphingolipid levels in plasma and cerebrospinal fluid of individuals with Lysosomal Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: These results reveal dysregulated GSL metabolism and suggest the potential of gangliosides as biomarkers.

readouts.interpretation: Altered glycosphingolipid levels report secondary GSL metabolic dysregulation in FSASD.

nodes.description: Hepatosplenomegaly is a severe infantile FSASD manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Severe FSASD (historically referred to as infantile free sialic acid storage disease, or ISSD) is characterized by severe developmental delay, coarse facial features, hepatosplenomegaly, and cardiomegaly; death usually occurs in early childhood.

subtypes: Infantile Free Sialic Acid Storage Disease

nodes.description: Muscular hypotonia is part of both infantile and intermediate/severe presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Free sialic acid storage disorder: Progress and promise.
evidence.snippet: Clinical features of FSASD include coarse facial features, organomegaly, and progressive neurodegenerative symptoms with cognitive impairment, cerebellar ataxia and muscular hypotonia.

evidence.reference_title: A New Patient With Intermediate Severe Salla Disease With Hypomyelination: A Literature Review for Salla Disease.
evidence.snippet: PATIENT DESCRIPTION: This five-year-old girl presented with infantile-onset severe global developmental delay, truncal hypotonia, and generalized dystonia following normal development during her first six months of life.

nodes.description: Cognitive impairment is common in the Salla disease end of the FSASD spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Psychiatric symptoms in Salla disease.
evidence.snippet: Salla disease (SD) is a rare lysosomal storage disorder characterised by intellectual disability ataxia, athetosis, nystagmus, and central nervous system demyelination.

nodes.description: Free N-acetylneuraminic acid accumulates in lysosomes and is measurable in urine, CSF, leukocytes, and fibroblasts. Higher urinary or intracellular free sialic acid burden predicts a more severe disease course.

evidence.reference_title: Free sialic acid storage disorder: Progress and promise.
evidence.snippet: SLC17A5 defects cause free sialic acid and some other acidic hexoses to accumulate in lysosomes, resulting in enlarged lysosomes in some cell types and 10-100-fold increased urinary excretion of free sialic acid.

evidence.reference_title: Investigating the Utility of Leukocyte Sialic Acid Measurements in Lysosomal Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with FSASD exhibited significantly higher levels of free Neu5Ac compared to their unaffected biological parents (36-fold), LSD-negative subjects (22-fold), and individuals with other LSDs (49-fold).

evidence.reference_title: A cross-sectional quantitative analysis of the natural history of free sialic acid storage disease-an ultra-orphan multisystemic lysosomal storage disorder.
evidence.snippet: The biochemical phenotype clearly predicted the disease course: patients with a urinary free sialic acid excretion below 6.37-fold or an intracellular free sialic acid storage in fibroblasts below 7.37-fold of the mean of normal survived longer than patients with biochemical values above these thresholds.

experimental_models.description: Patient-derived neural cells recapitulate elevated free sialic acid across differentiated neural cell types.
experimental_models.name: Patient-derived iPSC neural cell models

evidence.reference_title: Elevated CSF N-acetylaspartylglutamate in patients with free sialic acid storage diseases.
evidence.snippet: H-NMRS revealed an increase of N-acetylaspartylglutamate in the CSF of all patients with SLC17A5 mutation (range 13-114 micromol/L, reference <12 micromol/L).

readouts.interpretation: Elevated CSF NAAG supports SLC17A5-related sialin transport dysfunction in hypomyelinating disease.

nodes.description: CNS involvement causes delayed development, cognitive impairment, ataxia, hypotonia, spasticity, athetosis, seizures, and behavioral or psychiatric manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Free sialic acid storage disorder: Progress and promise.
evidence.snippet: Clinical features of FSASD include coarse facial features, organomegaly, and progressive neurodegenerative symptoms with cognitive impairment, cerebellar ataxia and muscular hypotonia.

evidence.reference_title: Psychiatric symptoms in Salla disease.
evidence.snippet: Psychiatric symptoms were frequently associated with SD (10/24, 42%), and two patients were described as developing psychosis as adolescents.

nodes.description: Nonimmune hydrops fetalis is a prenatal presentation of severe infantile FSASD.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal hydrops fetalis associated with infantile free sialic acid storage disease due to a novel homozygous deletion in the SLC17A5 gene.
evidence.snippet: We describe a case of fetal hydrops due to a novel homozygous deletion in the SLC17A5 gene.

subtypes: Infantile Free Sialic Acid Storage Disease

nodes.description: Nystagmus is a characteristic neurologic feature of Salla disease.

evidence.reference_title: Psychiatric symptoms in Salla disease.
evidence.snippet: Salla disease (SD) is a rare lysosomal storage disorder characterised by intellectual disability ataxia, athetosis, nystagmus, and central nervous system demyelination.

nodes.description: iPSC-derived radial glial cells, immature and mature astrocytes, and cortical neurons from individuals with FSASD model free sialic acid accumulation and neural-cell GSL dysregulation.

evidence.reference_title: Lysosomal free sialic acid storage disorder iPSC-derived neural cells display altered glycosphingolipid metabolism.
evidence.snippet: Our findings revealed significant elevations in free sialic acid levels across all FSASD cell types, indicating that iPSCs and derived iRGCs, iIAs, iMAs and iCNs may be used to model FSASD in vitro.

nodes.description: Psychotic symptoms have been reported in adolescent Salla disease patients.

evidence.reference_title: Psychiatric symptoms in Salla disease.
evidence.snippet: Psychiatric symptoms were frequently associated with SD (10/24, 42%), and two patients were described as developing psychosis as adolescents.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of FSASD is established in a proband by identification of biallelic pathogenic variants in SLC17A5 by molecular genetic testing.

evidence.reference_title: The spectrum of SLC17A5-gene mutations resulting in free sialic acid-storage diseases indicates some genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: Ten different mutations, including deletions, insertions, and missense and nonsense mutations, were identified in patients with the most severe ISSD phenotype, most of whom were compound heterozygotes.

nodes.description: Biallelic SLC17A5 variants reduce or abolish sialin transporter function across the free sialic acid storage disease spectrum. Genotype contributes to severity: homozygous R39C is associated with milder Salla disease, while other truncating, deletion, missense, and compound-heterozygous genotypes can produce severe infantile or intermediate phenotypes.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of FSASD is established in a proband by identification of biallelic pathogenic variants in SLC17A5 by molecular genetic testing.

evidence.reference_title: The spectrum of SLC17A5-gene mutations resulting in free sialic acid-storage diseases indicates some genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: Our results indicate some genotype-phenotype correlation in free sialic acid-storage diseases, suggesting that the phenotype associated with the homozygote R39C mutation is milder than that associated with other mutations.

nodes.description: Reported homozygous deletion causing infantile FSASD with prenatal hydrops.
Epileptic seizures can occur with progressive neurologic disease.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Less severe FSASD (historically called Salla disease) is characterized by normal appearance and absence of neurologic findings at birth, followed by slowly progressive neurologic deterioration resulting in mild-to-moderate psychomotor delays, spasticity, athetosis, and epileptic seizures.

nodes.description: Sialin is the lysosomal sialic acid exporter. Pathogenic variants impair proton-coupled sialic acid transport, with recent cryo-EM structures identifying residues and conformational states that explain mutation effects and substrate recognition.

evidence.reference_title: Base editing corrects the common Salla disease SLC17A5 c.115C>T variant.
evidence.snippet: Loss or deficiency of sialin impairs FSA transport out of the lysosome, leading to cellular dysfunction and neurological impairment, with the most severe form of FSASD resulting in death during early childhood.

evidence.reference_title: The molecular mechanism of sialic acid transport mediated by Sialin.
evidence.snippet: By mapping known pathogenic mutations, we provide mechanistic explanations for corresponding functional defects.

evidence.reference_title: Structure and inhibition of the human lysosomal transporter Sialin.
evidence.snippet: Thus, our findings uncover the essential conformational states in NAAG and sialic acid transport, demonstrating a working model of SLC17 transporters.

nodes.description: Sleep disorders are relatively common psychiatric/behavioral manifestations in Salla disease.

evidence.reference_title: Psychiatric symptoms in Salla disease.
evidence.snippet: Other psychiatric factors associated with SD were sleeping disorders (8/24, 32%)

nodes.description: Slowly progressive neurologic deterioration can lead to limb spasticity.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Less severe FSASD (historically called Salla disease) is characterized by normal appearance and absence of neurologic findings at birth, followed by slowly progressive neurologic deterioration resulting in mild-to-moderate psychomotor delays, spasticity, athetosis, and epileptic seizures.

nodes.description: Current care is symptomatic and supportive, including seizure management, developmental and educational support, rehabilitation, nutrition and feeding support, ophthalmologic care, cardiology and nephrology surveillance when needed, and family support.

evidence.reference_title: Free Sialic Acid Storage Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Treatment of manifestations: Management is symptomatic and supportive: standard treatment of seizures; developmental and educational support; rehabilitation to optimize mobility; supplementation of calcium and vitamin D for low bone density; feeding therapy and provision of adequate nutrition; treatment of ophthalmologic manifestations per ophthalmologist with low vision services as needed; treatment of cardiomegaly per cardiologist; treatment of nephropathy / nephrotic syndrome per nephrologist; surgical treatment of hernia as needed; family and social support.

evidence.reference_title: Free sialic acid storage disorder: Progress and promise.
evidence.snippet: There is no approved therapy for FSASD.

nodes.description: Brain MRI often shows central hypomyelination and related white matter abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Free sialic acid storage disorder: Progress and promise.
evidence.snippet: Central hypomyelination with cerebellar atrophy and thinning of the corpus callosum are also prominent disease features.

evidence.reference_title: Lysosomal free sialic acid storage disorder iPSC-derived neural cells display altered glycosphingolipid metabolism.
evidence.snippet: While the involvement of oligodendroglia in FSASD pathogenesis is established, the roles of other neural cell types remain elusive.

nodes.description: Finnish founder variant associated with milder Salla disease when homozygous.
